name: system-watchdog
description: Nightly system anomaly check at 4 AM. Runs check.sh, alerts only on actionable anomalies (memory pressure, runaway processes, disk pressure). Silent when everything is normal.

payload.message:
System watchdog check. Follow these steps:

1. Read the skill doc:
   Read file: ~/.openclaw/skills/system-watchdog/SKILL.md

2. Run the check script:
   Execute: bash ~/.openclaw/skills/system-watchdog/check.sh

3. Parse the JSON output. Look at the 'suspicious' field.

4. If suspicious is FALSE: Reply with just SKIP — do NOT send any message.

5. If suspicious is TRUE: Format a concise report following the format in SKILL.md.
   Lead with the verdict and 1-3 key findings.
   Do NOT report: process age alone, Docker/virtualization baseline usage, or absolute disk usage unless near a real limit.
   Use the message tool to send the report to your configured channel.

Keep it short and scannable. Every alert should explain what changed and why it matters.
